deal.Acquirer: Buyer Group (a consortium of funds advised by BC Partners, Inc., La Caisse de dépôt et placement du Québec, affiliates of GIC Special Investments Pte Ltd, affiliates of StepStone Group LP and Longview Asset Management, LLC)

bidder_21.resolved_name: La Caisse de dépôt et placement du Québec
aliases_observed: La Caisse de dépôt et placement du Québec

deal_flags.reason: 27 potential participants contacted JPM (3 strategics ex-IP + 24 financials); only 15 signed NDAs. The 12 other contacts gave no price or bid intent per §M1 and are not emitted.
deal_flags.source_quote: During the period from the middle of August through the end of October, the Company developed the Profit Improvement Plan and prepared for a sale process. During this period, J.P. Morgan was contacted by 27 potential participants in a sale process, including three strategic parties (not counting Industry Participant) and 24 financial participants (including potential lead buyers as well as large suppliers of equity capital to lead buyers).

_unnamed_nda_promotions.reason: Buyer Group is one of the 15 financial buyers that executed NDAs in first week of October 2014 (p.30); Unnamed party 5's NDA row promoted to Buyer Group.
Bidder 2 is one of the 15 financial buyers that executed NDAs in first week of October 2014; Unnamed party 6's NDA row promoted to Bidder 2.

events.additional_note: Target-initiated exploratory contact with Industry Participant (a privately-held industry participant) pre-dates the sale resolution.
events.source_quote: In March, 2014, the board authorized management to contact Industry Participant to determine Industry Participant’s interest in initiating exploratory discussions concerning the feasibility of a merger or acquisition transaction.

events.additional_note: JANA filed Schedule 13D disclosing 9.9% stake and intent to advocate for sale.
events.source_quote: On July 3, 2014, JANA Partners filed a Schedule 13D with the SEC disclosing that JANA had acquired 9.9% of the Company’s outstanding common stock and that JANA intended to engage in discussions with management and the board with respect to a review of strategic alternatives, including a sale of the Company.

flags.reason: §D1.b: separate Activist Sale rows for JANA and Longview (separately narrated activists).

events.additional_note: Longview made public a letter advocating board consider a sale.
events.source_quote: On July 7, 2014, Longview made public a letter to the board in which Longview stated that the board should consider the a sale of the Company (as well as other strategic alternatives) and that should the board determine a sale transaction to be in the interests of all stockholders, Longview would consider, depending on the parties and terms involved, rolling part or all of its holdings into equity of the acquiring entity rather than receive cash consideration, should doing so prove necessary in order to enable the Company to complete a transaction.

events.additional_note: Target's financial advisor; month-only date inferred per §B1; engagement letter elsewhere in filing (p.37) dated August 21, 2014 but Background narrates July retention.
events.source_quote: In July, after interviewing several potential financial advisors, the Company retained J.P. Morgan as financial advisor based on, among other factors, J.P. Morgan’s reputation, experience in mergers and acquisitions, valuation, financing and capital markets and its familiarity with the Company.

events.additional_note: Industry Participant contacted J.P. Morgan expressing possible interest in revisiting prior discussions; no NDA signed, no concrete price.
events.source_quote: On August 7, 2014, a representative of Industry Participant contacted a representative of J.P. Morgan. The Industry Participant representative indicated that she had heard rumors that J.P. Morgan was working with the Company and wanted to inform J.P. Morgan that, if the Company were to pursue strategic alternatives, Industry Participant might be interested in re-visiting the conversations that had taken place in the Spring concerning the feasibility of a possible combination of the two Companies.

events.additional_note: Board determined to explore strategic alternatives including a sale of the Company.
events.source_quote: On August 13, 2014, the board met in person, together with members of management and representatives of J.P. Morgan and Wachtell Lipton, to complete its initial review of strategic and financial alternatives potentially available to the Company and to receive an update on communications from stockholders and other interested parties, including Industry Participant. As a result of this meeting, the board (1) determined to explore strategic alternatives (including a possible sale of the Company) for the Company to maximize value for stockholders including by commencing a process to determine the potential value that could be achieved via a sale of the Company

events.additional_note: Public announcement via press release concurrent with Q2 earnings.
events.source_quote: On August 19, 2014, concurrent with its second quarter earnings announcement, the Company issued a press release announcing that it determined to explore strategic alternatives for the Company to maximize value for stockholders, including a possible sale of the Company.

events.additional_note: Press release announcing exploration of strategic alternatives including possible sale.
events.source_quote: On August 19, 2014, concurrent with its second quarter earnings announcement, the Company issued a press release announcing that it determined to explore strategic alternatives for the Company to maximize value for stockholders, including a possible sale of the Company.

events.additional_note: Target excluded Industry Participant from the sale process for antitrust / due-diligence / process-disruption concerns.
events.source_quote: On August 27, 2014, representatives of Industry Participant contacted by telephone the representative of J.P. Morgan. During this call, the Industry Participant representatives stated that Industry Participant would have interest in discussing the possibility of a combination of the two companies. The J.P. Morgan representative reiterated the concerns identified by the board that engaging with Industry Participant was unlikely to maximize value for PetSmart’s stockholders. The J.P. Morgan representative stated that Industry Participant would not be invited into the exploratory process, but that if Industry Participant were to wish to submit a proposal or other communication to the Company, the board would consider it. There has been no contact between PetSmart and Industry Participant (or between any of their respective representatives) since August 27, 2014.

events.additional_note: 1 of 15 confidentiality and standstill agreements with financial buyers; specific signer not narrated by filing.
events.source_quote: In the first week of October 2014, the Company entered into confidentiality and standstill agreements with 15 potentially interested financial buyers, all 15 of which, during the month of October engaged in due diligence, received management presentations involving the Company’s senior-most management, and received detailed financial and business plan information, including detailed information concerning the Profit Improvement Plan and associated cost-saving plans and opportunities.

events.additional_note: Informal round (non-binding indications) announced; deadline October 30, 2014.
events.source_quote: During October, the potential bidders were informed that non-binding preliminary indications of interest would be due on October 30, 2014.

events.additional_note: Third of three bidders with initial indication ≥$80 per share; exact range not narrated; recorded as lower-only bound per §H1.
events.source_quote: Three bidders initially indicated price ranges that reached at least $80.00 per share, including the Buyer Group, which indicated a range of $81.00 to $83.00 per share, and another bidder, which suggested a range of $80.00 to $85.00 per share.

flags.reason: Filing discloses ≥$80 per share but not upper bound for this bidder.
informal per §G1: non-binding 'preliminary indications of interest' round; bid submitted in response to Final Round Inf Ann.

events.bid_type_inference_note: informal per §G1: 'non-binding preliminary indications of interest' round; bid pre-NDA-binding documentation; no financing commitments, no merger-agreement markup.

events.additional_note: One of two below-$80 submitters who did not raise after J.P. Morgan discussions; specific price not narrated.
events.source_quote: From October 30 to November 2, 2014, representatives of J.P. Morgan spoke by telephone with all of the potentially interested parties, including those that did not submit an indication of interest, to hear the parties’ respective rationales for the price levels suggested in their indications or rationales for not submitting an indication.

flags.reason: Filing does not state this bidder's specific Oct 30 indication price.

events.bid_type_inference_note: informal per §G1: submitted in Final Round Inf (non-binding indications round).

events.source_quote: From October 30 to November 2, 2014, representatives of J.P. Morgan spoke by telephone with all of the potentially interested parties, including those that did not submit an indication of interest, to hear the parties’ respective rationales for the price levels suggested in their indications or rationales for not submitting an indication.

events.additional_note: 'another bidder' range $80–$85 per share.
events.source_quote: Three bidders initially indicated price ranges that reached at least $80.00 per share, including the Buyer Group, which indicated a range of $81.00 to $83.00 per share, and another bidder, which suggested a range of $80.00 to $85.00 per share.

events.additional_note: Buyer Group initial indication range $81–$83 per share; later wins with $83.00.
events.source_quote: Three bidders initially indicated price ranges that reached at least $80.00 per share, including the Buyer Group, which indicated a range of $81.00 to $83.00 per share, and another bidder, which suggested a range of $80.00 to $85.00 per share.

events.additional_note: Bidder 2 initial October 30 indication of $78.00 per share; subsequently revised to $81-$84 after J.P. Morgan discussions.
events.source_quote: As a result of its discussions with J.P. Morgan, another bidder (which we refer to as “Bidder 2”), which had initially indicated a price of $78.00, increased its indication to a range of $81.00 to $84.00 per share.
events.bid_type_inference_note: informal per §G1: Final Round Inf (non-binding indications).

events.bid_date_rough: revision during Oct 30–Nov 2, 2014 J.P. Morgan discussions; anchored to Oct 30 initial-indication date
events.additional_note: Bidder 2 revision after J.P. Morgan discussions (Oct 30–Nov 2): range $81–$84 per share.

flags.reason: revision occurred during Oct 30–Nov 2 J.P. Morgan conversations; anchored to Oct 30 for same-date treatment.

events.bid_type_inference_note: informal per §G1: Final Round Inf revision; still non-binding indication.

events.additional_note: Board determined to allow four bidders (≥$80 per share indications) to proceed to the final round.
events.source_quote: On November 3, 2014, the board met in person, together with members of management and representatives of J.P. Morgan and Wachtell Lipton, to continue its review of strategic alternatives, including a review of the indications of interest. The board determined to allow the four bidders that had indicated a price or range at or above $80.00 per share to proceed to the final round of the sale process.

events.additional_note: Target-initiated cut at informal stage: submitted <$80 indication and did not raise; not invited to final round.
events.source_quote: Following this meeting, representatives of J.P. Morgan notified the eliminated parties, none of which indicated any interest or ability to remain in the process at price levels above their respective initial indications.

events.additional_note: Target-initiated cut at informal stage: submitted <$80 indication and did not raise; not invited to final round.
events.source_quote: Following this meeting, representatives of J.P. Morgan notified the eliminated parties, none of which indicated any interest or ability to remain in the process at price levels above their respective initial indications.

events.additional_note: Ad hoc committee instructed J.P. Morgan to request revised bids by Dec 12.
events.source_quote: On December 10, 2014, the ad hoc committee discussed the bids and the draft merger agreements with representatives of J.P. Morgan and Wachtell Lipton. The ad hoc committee was of the view that there could be no assurance that the board would approve either bid unless improved. In light of the closeness of the bids, recent positive developments identified at the board meeting on December 3, and the view shared by the ad hoc committee and J.P. Morgan that neither of the bids represented the bidders’ respective best offers, the ad hoc committee instructed J.P. Morgan to inform each bidder that it would need to increase its bid, and to instruct the bidders to submit improved bids on December 12, 2014.

events.additional_note: Consortium formed from two final-round invitees who requested permission to work together; verbal indication only, no written offer submitted.
events.source_quote: Bidder 3 verbally communicated to J.P. Morgan that their valuation would not be above the current stock price of approximately $78 per share. J.P. Morgan communicated to Bidder 3 that it was unlikely to be competitive and accordingly Bidder 3 did not submit a written offer.

flags.reason: Filing: 'valuation would not be above... approximately $78 per share' — single upper bound.
Bidder 3 is consortium of two bidders who worked together (constituents were originally invited as independent final-round bidders).

events.bid_type_inference_note: informal per §G1: verbal indication only, no written offer, no merger-agreement markup, no financing commitments; Bidder 3 did not participate in the formal final round.

events.additional_note: Final-round formal bid: $80.70/share in cash, with executed merger-agreement markup and financing commitment documents.
events.source_quote: On December 10, PetSmart received final bid letters along with revised versions of the merger agreement and other transaction documents from the Buyer Group and from Bidder 2 and a verbal indication from Bidder 3. The Buyer Group offered $80.70 per share, in cash, and Bidder 2 offered $80.35 per share, in cash.
events.bid_type_inference_note: formal per §G1: final-round bid with merger-agreement markup, financing commitment documents (per 12/6 passage 'Buyer Group provided its financing commitment documents'), responsive to final-round process.

events.additional_note: Final-round formal bid: $80.35/share in cash, with merger-agreement markup.
events.source_quote: On December 10, PetSmart received final bid letters along with revised versions of the merger agreement and other transaction documents from the Buyer Group and from Bidder 2 and a verbal indication from Bidder 3. The Buyer Group offered $80.70 per share, in cash, and Bidder 2 offered $80.35 per share, in cash.
events.bid_type_inference_note: formal per §G1: final-round bid with merger-agreement markup responsive to final-round process; final-round structural signal.

events.additional_note: Bidder 3 constituent A; target-initiated cut after verbal ~$78 was communicated to be unlikely to be competitive; no written formal offer submitted.
events.source_quote: Bidder 3 verbally communicated to J.P. Morgan that their valuation would not be above the current stock price of approximately $78 per share. J.P. Morgan communicated to Bidder 3 that it was unlikely to be competitive and accordingly Bidder 3 did not submit a written offer.

flags.reason: Bidder 3 consortium dropped; row split per constituent NDA per §I1.

events.additional_note: Bidder 3 constituent B; target-initiated cut after verbal ~$78 was communicated to be unlikely to be competitive; no written formal offer submitted.
events.source_quote: Bidder 3 verbally communicated to J.P. Morgan that their valuation would not be above the current stock price of approximately $78 per share. J.P. Morgan communicated to Bidder 3 that it was unlikely to be competitive and accordingly Bidder 3 did not submit a written offer.

flags.reason: Bidder 3 consortium dropped; row split per constituent NDA per §I1.

events.additional_note: Deadline for submission of final bids (set Dec 5 as Dec 10, per ad hoc committee); three final-round bidders: Buyer Group, Bidder 2, Bidder 3 (two-bidder consortium).
events.source_quote: On December 10, PetSmart received final bid letters along with revised versions of the merger agreement and other transaction documents from the Buyer Group and from Bidder 2 and a verbal indication from Bidder 3.

events.additional_note: Best and final offer from Bidder 2: $81.50/share cash.
events.source_quote: During the evening of December 12, 2014, Bidder 2 submitted an offer of $81.50 per share, in cash. Representatives of J.P. Morgan confirmed via a conversation with Bidder 2 on the evening of December 12, 2014 that $81.50 per share was its best and final offer.
events.bid_type_inference_note: formal per §G1: 'best and final' trigger phrase; final-round extension bid with merger-agreement markup and financing commitment documents.

events.additional_note: Buyer Group initial oral offer on Dec 12 at $82.50/share; stated intent to increase; superseded later same day by $83.00 best-and-final.
events.source_quote: The Buyer Group initially submitted an oral offer of $82.50 per share, in cash, but stated that it was working to increase the offer within the next few hours.
events.bid_type_inference_note: formal per §G1: final-round extension bid; merger-agreement markup and financing commitments per 12/12 passage; oral preceded written best-and-final; same-date multi-communication per §C3.

events.additional_note: Buyer Group best-and-final: $83.00/share cash; 'with few exceptions, in substantially executable form.' Winning bid.
events.source_quote: Later in the evening, the Buyer Group submitted a best and final offer of $83.00 per share, in cash. In addition, each bidder submitted a revised draft of the merger agreement and other transaction agreements, and copies of their respective financing commitment documents. The versions submitted by the Buyer Group were, with few exceptions, in substantially executable form.
events.bid_type_inference_note: formal per §G1: 'best and final offer' trigger phrase; final-round bid with substantially-executable merger-agreement markup and financing commitment documents.

events.additional_note: Deadline for revised and improved final bids.
events.source_quote: the ad hoc committee instructed J.P. Morgan to inform each bidder that it would need to increase its bid, and to instruct the bidders to submit improved bids on December 12, 2014.

events.additional_note: Target rejected Bidder 2's best-and-final $81.50 in favor of Buyer Group's $83.00; Bidder 2 stated $81.50 was its best and final.
events.source_quote: On December 13, 2014, the board met in person, together with the members of management and representatives of J.P. Morgan and Wachtell Lipton, to discuss and review the final bids and to consider the proposed transaction. Representatives of J.P. Morgan reviewed the respective bids as well as the recent progression of the sale process. Representatives of Wachtell Lipton reviewed the terms of the draft merger agreement and other transaction documents and reviewed the financing commitments that had been provided by the Buyer Group. Representatives of J.P. Morgan made a financial presentation concerning the proposed transaction with the Buyer Group, as well as the Company’s alternatives to a sale of the Company, including a leveraged share repurchase.

events.additional_note: Merger agreement, voting agreement, and related transaction agreements executed; $83.00/share cash; Parent (Argos Holdings Inc.) is the legal counterparty owned by the Buyer Group consortium (BC Partners, La Caisse, GIC, StepStone, Longview).
source_quote: Following the board meeting, the parties completed negotiations on terms satisfactory to both parties, and finalized the terms of the Longview voting agreement. On December 14, 2014, the parties executed the merger agreement, the voting agreement and related transaction agreements and issued a press release announcing the transaction.

the “Buyer Group” refers to a consortium of funds advised by BC Partners, Inc., La Caisse de dépôt et placement du Québec, affiliates of GIC Special Investments Pte Ltd, affiliates of StepStone Group LP and, at all times after December 12, 2014, Longview Asset Management, LLC;
